human_verification.notes: Reviewed the package for home-services analytics boundaries, service-resolution behavior, metric-definition rigor, OASIS and quality evidence, PHI and minimum-necessary controls, payer/provider dependencies, clinical-versus-administrative escalation behavior, functionality coverage, and validated spec-pack completeness.

review_notes: All required scenario families for the issue and healthcare-and-care-delivery research contract are covered and mapped to passing results.
All required functional abilities for service-support-and-resolution plus healthcare-and-care-delivery are represented and validated.

specialist_owned_evidence: Boundary is explicit to home-services analytics service resolution, metric definition, source hierarchy, denominator and exclusion logic, reconciliation notes, caveat design, PHI-safe handling, and adjacent-authority escalation rather than generic BI or customer support work.

Authority sources cover home-health conditions of participation, CMS home-health benefit and claims workflow, CMS prior authorization, HHS minimum-necessary controls, CMS HH QRP, OASIS data specifications, Home Health Star Ratings, and ONC USCDI terminology.

Evaluation scenarios cover normal in-scope analysis, missing request detail, missing authorization/referral/eligibility dependencies, PHI-sensitive requests, clinical and payer boundaries, policy exceptions, adjacent-lane handoff, conflicting systems, unsupported stronger claims, ineligible requests, repeat failure escalation, tenant adaptation, and home-services terminology handling.
Runtime remains specification-based with explicit promotion requirements for any later retained-implementation transition.
notes: No blocking unresolved ambiguity remains; tenant-specific metric definitions, jurisdictional privacy rules, payer contracts, source-precedence rules, and local reporting differences are handled through runtime retrieval and escalation rules.
This package is ready for validated operations under specification-based execution controls.
